action: Create a privacy-minimal capture adapter for physical iPhone audio evidence that binds gesture-time activation, runtime state, and human outcome into one bounded session.
selection_basis: The canonical queue contains no active frontier_research item. Recent Frontier artifacts addressed classification, lifecycle composition, replay, and deployment/session binding; the remaining implementation handoff was a minimal capture adapter that produces the required ordered evidence without collecting unnecessary payloads.

progress.finding: Transient activation must be captured synchronously at gesture time, while runtime observations and a human heard/not_heard report must remain distinct and temporally ordered. None alone proves audibility.
progress.verification: 7 deterministic Node tests passed in an isolated runtime using byte-identical pre-commit files
progress.privacy: Output is allowlisted; free-form notes, analyser arrays, and unrelated activation/device fields are excluded.

handoff: Vercel Studio should invoke createAudioCaptureSession directly inside the diagnostic pulse gesture, append runtime state after the pulse starts, append a binary heard/not_heard response from the same physical iPhone session, then pass the packet through the existing classifier and deployment/session binding validator.
limits: Not wired into the deployed UI.
No immutable Vercel deployment identity available in this run.
Adapter structure is not cryptographic proof and does not establish claim truth.
shared_state_policy: Additive queue update only; operations/ACTIVE_QUEUE.json, operations/CURRENT_STATE.json, operations/SPECIALIST_REGISTRY.json, and other teams' records were not overwritten.
